text.title: AI+Human Content Creation Workflow
text.summary: Six-stage content production workflow where AI handles research, outline, draft, and optimization while humans add first-hand experience, proprietary data, brand voice, and QA — with human approval gates before scheduling.

rule.statement: AI handles: keyword research, topic brainstorming, outline generation, first drafts, SEO metadata, content repurposing variants. Humans handle: strategy, expert insights, first-hand experience, brand voice enforcement, fact verification, proprietary data injection.
rule.rationale: AI aggregates and synthesizes existing web content efficiently. Humans provide what AI cannot generate: original research, lived experience, and genuine opinions. The combination produces content Google's E-E-A-T system rewards; AI-only content does not.

rule.statement: Never "prompt and publish." Every AI draft requires human review and enrichment before scheduling.
rule.rationale: AI-generated content without human enrichment produces averaged internet output that ranks poorly and fails E-E-A-T criteria. Automated publication pipelines must include a human approval gate before the publish step.

list.item: Stage 1 (AI): Keyword research, competitor analysis, initial outline, structure suggestion, word count estimate.
Stage 2 (Human): Add first-hand experiences, insert proprietary data, include specific case studies, add expert quotes.
Stage 3 (AI): Generate full first draft from enriched outline, add internal links, suggest metadata.
Stage 4 (Human): Enforce brand voice, add opinions and contrarian takes, verify all claims and data, simplify complex sections.
Stage 5 (AI): SEO optimization via Surfer/MarketMuse, generate meta descriptions, create social media variants.
Stage 6 (Human): Fact-check all claims, verify links, check formatting, final brand review, approve for publication.

rule.statement: In automated content pipelines, human approval must be a hard gate before the publish step — not an optional notification.
rule.rationale: AI content pipelines can publish at a rate that exceeds human review capacity. Without a hard gate, defective content (hallucinated statistics, brand voice violations) reaches publication at scale.

example.code: [New topic brief] → AI: generate outline → Human approval: approve/reject → IF approved: AI: generate draft → Surfer: score → AI: apply top 3 SEO suggestions → Human approval: review draft → IF approved: schedule to CMS → notify team

text.reference: AI content prompt template with role, constraints, context, task, output format
